info.name: Sirach (Ecclesiasticus)
info.desc: The Wisdom of Jesus son of Sirach (Ecclesiasticus)

1:
All wisdom comes from the Lord,
and Wisdom is with him for ever.
In Praise of Wisdom
; Prov 2.6
2:
Who can count raindrops or the sand along the shore?
Who can count the days of eternity?
In Praise of Wisdom
3:
How high is the sky? How wide is the earth?
How deep is the ocean? How profound is Wisdom?
Can anyone find answers to these questions?
In Praise of Wisdom
4:
Wisdom was created before anything else;
understanding has always existed.
In Praise of Wisdom
; 1.4 Some manuscripts add verse 5: The source of Wisdom is the word of God on high; her ways are eternal commands.
6:
Has anyone ever been shown where Wisdom originates?
Does anyone understand her subtle cleverness?
In Praise of Wisdom
; 1.6 Some manuscripts add verse 7: To whom has the knowledge of Wisdom been revealed? Who has understood her great experience?
8:
There is only one who is wise,
and we must stand in awe before his throne.
In Praise of Wisdom
9: In Praise of Wisdom
10:
He gave some measure of Wisdom to everyone,
but poured her out on those who love him.
In Praise of Wisdom
11:
If you fear the Lord, honour and pride will be yours;
you will be crowned with happiness and joy.
In Praise of Wisdom
12:
To honour the Lord is a heartfelt delight;
it will give you a long and happy life,
In Praise of Wisdom
13:
and at the end of your days all will go well for you.
God will bless you on the day of your death.
In Praise of Wisdom
14:
To fear the Lord is the first step to Wisdom.
Wisdom is given to the faithful in their mothers' wombs.
In Praise of Wisdom
15:
She has lived with us from ancient times,
and generations to come will rely on her.
In Praise of Wisdom
16:
To fear the Lord is Wisdom at her fullest;
she satisfies us completely with her gifts
In Praise of Wisdom
17:
and fills our homes and barns
with all that our hearts can desire.
In Praise of Wisdom
18:
To fear the Lord is the flower of Wisdom
that blossoms with peace and good health.
In Praise of Wisdom
; 1.18 Some manuscripts add: He saw her and recognized her value (see 1.9).
19:
She sends knowledge and understanding like the rain,
and increases the honour of those who receive her.
In Praise of Wisdom
20:
To fear the Lord is the root of Wisdom;
her branches are long life.
Self-control
1.20 Some manuscripts add verse 21: Honouring the Lord takes sin away; where the fear of the Lord is found, it turns away anger.; Prov 3.16; 4.10
22: There is no excuse for unjustified anger; it can bring about your downfall.
Self-control
23: Wait and be patient, and later you will be glad you did.
Self-control
24: Keep quiet until the right time to speak, and you will gain a reputation for good sense.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
25: Wisdom has a treasury of wise sayings, but sinners have nothing but contempt for godliness.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
26: If you want to be wise, keep the Lord's commands, and he will give you Wisdom in abundance.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
27: Fearing the Lord is Wisdom and an education in itself. He is pleased by loyalty and humility.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
28: Be faithful in the practice of your religion; when you worship the Lord, do it with all your heart.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
29: Be careful about what you say, and don't be a hypocrite.
Wisdom and Reverence for God
30: Don't be arrogant; you may suffer a fall and be disgraced. The Lord will reveal your secrets and humble you in front of everyone in the synagogue, because you did not come there with reverence for the Lord, but with a heart full of hypocrisy.
Wisdom and Reverence for God

1: My child, if you are going to serve the Lord, be prepared for times when you will be put to the test.
Faithfulness to God
2: Be sincere and determined. Keep calm when trouble comes.
Faithfulness to God
3: Stay with the Lord; never abandon him, and you will be prosperous at the end of your days.
Faithfulness to God
4: Accept whatever happens to you. Even if you suffer humiliation, be patient.
Faithfulness to God
5: Gold is tested by fire, and human character is tested in the furnace of humiliation.
Faithfulness to God
6: Trust the Lord, and he will help you. Walk straight in his ways, and put your hope in him.
Faithfulness to God
7: All you that fear the Lord, wait for him to show you his mercy. Do not turn away from him, or you will fall.
Faithfulness to God
8: All you that fear the Lord, trust him, and you will certainly be rewarded.
Faithfulness to God
9: All you that fear the Lord, look forward to his blessings of mercy and eternal happiness.
Faithfulness to God
10: Think back to the ancient generations and consider this: has the Lord ever disappointed anyone who put his hope in him? Has the Lord ever abandoned anyone who held him in constant reverence? Has the Lord ever ignored anyone who prayed to him?
Faithfulness to God
11: The Lord is kind and merciful; he forgives our sins and keeps us safe in time of trouble.
Faithfulness to God
12: But those who lose their nerve are doomed — all those sinners who try to have it both ways!
Faithfulness to God
13: Doom is sure to come for those who lose their courage; they have no faith, and so they will have no protection.
Faithfulness to God
14: Doom is sure to come for those who lose their hope. What will they do when the Lord comes to judge them?
Faithfulness to God
15: Those who fear the Lord do not disobey his commands; those who love him will live as he wants them to live.
Faithfulness to God
16: Those who fear and love the Lord will try to please him and devote themselves to the Law.
Faithfulness to God
17: Those who fear the Lord are always ready to serve him. They humble themselves before him, and say,
Faithfulness to God
18: “We place our destiny in the hands of the Lord, not in human hands, because his mercy is as great as his majesty.”
Faithfulness to God

1: Children, listen to me; I am your father. Do what I tell you and you will be safe,
Duties to Parents
2: for the Lord has given fathers authority over their children and given children the obligation to obey their mothers.
Duties to Parents
3: If you respect your father, you can make up for your sins,
Duties to Parents
4: and if you honour your mother, you are earning great wealth.
Duties to Parents
5: If you respect your father, one day your own children will make you happy; the Lord will hear your prayers.
Duties to Parents
6: If you obey the Lord by honouring your father and making your mother happy, you will live a long life.
Duties to Parents
7: Obey your parents as if you were their slave.
Duties to Parents
8: Honour your father in everything you do and say, so that you may receive his blessing.
Duties to Parents
9: When parents give their blessing, they give strength to their children's homes, but when they curse their children, they destroy the very foundations.
Duties to Parents
10: Never seek honour for yourself at your father's expense; it is not to your credit if he is dishonoured.
Duties to Parents
11: Your own honour comes from the respect that you show to your father. If children do not honour their mothers, it is their own disgrace.
Duties to Parents
12: My child, take care of your father when he grows old; give him no cause for worry as long as he lives.
Duties to Parents
13: Be sympathetic even if his mind fails him; don't look down on him just because you are strong and healthy.
Duties to Parents
14: The Lord will not forget the kindness you show to your father; it will help you to make up for your sins.
Duties to Parents
15: When you are in trouble, the Lord will remember your kindness and will help you; your sins will melt away like frost in warm sunshine.
Duties to Parents
16: Those who abandon their parents or give them cause for anger may as well be cursing the Lord; they are already under the Lord's curse.
Humility
17: My child, be humble in everything you do, and people will appreciate it more than gifts.
Humility
3.17 Hebrew people… gifts; Greek those whom the Lord approves will love you.
18: The greater you become, the more humble you should be; then the Lord will be pleased with you.
Humility

3.18 Some manuscripts add verse 19: Many are exalted and esteemed, but God's secrets are revealed to the humble; Hebrew: God's mercy is great, and he reveals his secrets to those who are humble.; Phil 2.3
20: The Lord's power is great, and he is honoured by those who are humble.
Humility
21: Don't try to understand things that are too hard for you, or investigate matters that are beyond your power to know.
Humility
22: Concentrate on the Law, which has been given to you. You do not need to know about things which the Lord has not revealed,
Humility
Deut 29.29
23: so don't concern yourself with them. After all, what has been shown to you is beyond human power to understand.
Humility
24: Many people have been misled by their own opinions; their wrong ideas have warped their judgement.
Humility

3.24 Some manuscripts add verse 25: If you have no eyes, you cannot see; don't claim to have knowledge if you don't have it; Hebrew has, after verse 27: If you have no eyes, you cannot see; if you have no knowledge, you cannot have Wisdom.
26: Stubbornness will get you into trouble in the end. If you live dangerously, it will kill you.
Humility
27: A stubborn person will be burdened down with troubles. Sinners go on adding one sin to another.
Humility
28: There is no cure for the troubles that arrogant people have; wickedness has taken deep root in them.
Humility
29: Intelligent people will learn from proverbs and parables. They listen well because they want to learn.
Giving to the Poor
30: Giving to the poor can make up for sin, just as water can put out a blazing fire.
Giving to the Poor
31: Anyone who responds to others with acts of kindness is thinking of the future, because he will find help if he ever falls on hard times.
Giving to the Poor

1: My child, don't prevent the poor from making a living, or keep them waiting in their need.
2: Never give a hungry person any cause for resentment or anger.
3: Don't add to the troubles of someone who is already desperate. If he is in need, don't put off giving to him.
4-5: Don't refuse to help a beggar who is in distress. Don't turn your back on a poor person or give him any reason to curse you.
6: If he becomes so bitter that he does curse you, his Creator will hear his prayer.
7: Make yourself popular in the synagogue. Bow your head to men of authority.
8: Listen to what the poor have to say, and answer them politely.
9: Protect people from those who want to wrong them, and be firm in your judgements.
10: Be like a father to orphans, and provide widows with the help their husbands can no longer give them. Then you will be like a child of the Most High, and he will love you more than your own mother does.
Wisdom as a Teacher
11: Wisdom takes care of those who look for her; she raises them to greatness.
Wisdom as a Teacher
12: Loving her is loving life itself; rising early to look for her is pure joy.
Wisdom as a Teacher
13: Anyone who obtains Wisdom will be greatly honoured. Wherever he goes, the Lord will bless him.
Wisdom as a Teacher
14: Wisdom's servants are the servants of the Holy One, and the Lord loves everyone who loves her.
Wisdom as a Teacher
15:
Those who obey her will give sound judgements;
 those who pay attention to her have true security.
Wisdom as a Teacher
4.15 Hebrew give sound judgements; Greek judge nations.
16: Put your trust in Wisdom, and you will possess her and pass her on to your descendants.
Wisdom as a Teacher
17:
At first, Wisdom will lead you along difficult paths. She will make you so afraid that you will think you cannot go on. The discipline she demands will be tormenting, and she will put you to the test with her requirements until she trusts you
 completely.
Wisdom as a Teacher
4.17 she trusts you; or you trust her.
18: Then she will come to you with no delay, reveal her secrets to you, and make you happy.
Wisdom as a Teacher
19: But if you go astray, she will abandon you and let you go to your own ruin.
Self-confidence
20: Take advantage of opportunities, but guard yourself against evil. Don't under-rate yourself.
Self-confidence
21: Humility deserves honour and respect, but a low opinion of yourself leads to sin.
Self-confidence
22: Do not let others have their way at your expense; do not bring on your own ruin by giving up your rights.
Self-confidence
23:
Never hesitate to speak out when the occasion
 calls for it. Don't hide your wisdom.
Self-confidence

4.23 Hebrew Don't hide your wisdom; most Greek manuscripts do not have these words; other Greek manuscripts Don't hide your wisdom for the sake of appearances.; 4.23 Probable text the occasion; Greek safety.
24: Your wisdom and education can be known only by what you say.
Self-confidence
25: Do not, however, go against the truth, and remember that you do not know everything.
Self-confidence
26: Don't be ashamed to confess your sins; there's no point in trying to stop a river from flowing.
Self-confidence
27: Don't allow yourself to be dominated by someone who is stupid or show partiality to influential people.
Self-confidence
28: Stand up for what is right, even if it costs you your life; the Lord God will be fighting on your side.
Self-confidence
29: Don't be quick to speak or lazy and negligent in your work.
Self-confidence
30: Don't act like a lion at home or be suspicious of your servants.
Self-confidence
31: Don't stick out your hand to get something if you're going to be tightfisted when the time comes to pay it back.
Self-confidence
Acts 20.35

1: Don't rely on money to make you independent.
Don't Be Presumptuous
2: Don't think you have to have everything you want, and then spend your energy trying to get it.
Don't Be Presumptuous
3: Don't think that no one can exercise authority over you; if you do, the Lord is certain to punish you.
Don't Be Presumptuous
4: Don't think that you can sin and get away with it; the Lord does not mind waiting to punish you.
Don't Be Presumptuous
Ecc 8.11
5: Don't be so certain of the Lord's forgiveness that you go on committing one sin after another.
Don't Be Presumptuous
6: Don't think that his mercy is so great that he will forgive your sins no matter how many they are. He does show mercy, but he also shows his furious anger with sinners.
Don't Be Presumptuous
7: Come back to the Lord quickly. Don't think that you can keep putting it off. His anger can come upon you suddenly, and you will die under his punishment.
Don't Be Presumptuous
8: Don't rely on dishonest wealth; it will do you no good on that day of disaster.
Sincerity and Self-control
9-10: Be certain about what you believe and consistent in what you say. Don't try to please everyone or agree with everything people say.
5.9–10 Hebrew everything people say; Greek adds That is what double-tongued sinners do.
11: Always be ready to listen, but take your time in answering.
Sincerity and Self-control
12: Answer only if you know what to say, and if you don't know what to say, keep quiet.
Sincerity and Self-control
13: Speaking can bring you either honour or disgrace; what you say can ruin you.
Sincerity and Self-control
14: Don't get a reputation for being a gossip, and don't tell tales that will hurt people. Just as thieves will suffer disgrace, so liars will suffer severe condemnation.
Sincerity and Self-control
15:
Do nothing destructive,
 whether it seems insignificant or not,
Sincerity and Self-control
5.15 Hebrew Do nothing destructive; Greek Do not be ignorant in anything.

1: and do not be an enemy when you should be a friend. A bad reputation brings you the disgrace that lying sinners deserve.
2: Do not let your passions carry you away; this can tear your soul to pieces like a bull.
6.2 Verse 2 in both Greek and Hebrew is unclear.
3: You will be left like a dead tree without any leaves or fruit.
4: Evil desire will destroy you and make you a joke to your enemies.
Friendship
5: If you are polite and courteous, you will enjoy the friendship of many people.
Friendship
6: Exchange greetings with many, but take advice from only one person out of a thousand.
Friendship
7: When you make friends, don't be too quick to trust them; make sure that they have proved themselves.
Friendship
8: Some people will be your friends only when it is convenient for them, but they won't stand by you in trouble.
Friendship
9: Others will fall out with you over some argument, and then embarrass you by letting everyone know about it.
Friendship
10-11: Others will sit at your table as long as things are going well; they will stick to you like your shadow and give orders to your servants, but they will not stand by you in trouble.
12: If your situation takes a turn for the worse, they will turn against you, and you won't be able to find them anywhere.
Friendship
13: Stay away from your enemies and be on guard against your friends.
Friendship
14: A loyal friend is like a safe shelter; find one, and you have found a treasure.
Friendship
15: Nothing else is as valuable; there is no way of putting a price on it.
Friendship
16: A loyal friend is like a medicine that keeps you in good health. Only those who fear the Lord can find such a friend.
Friendship
17: A person who fears the Lord can make real friendships, because he will treat his friends as he does himself.
Learn from Wisdom
6.17 because… himself; or because his friends will also fear the Lord.
18: My child, learn to value Wisdom while you are young, and you will still be able to find her when you grow old.
Learn from Wisdom
19: Work as hard to find Wisdom as a farmer works to plough and sow his fields; then you can expect a good harvest. You will have to work at it for a while, but you will soon be enjoying what you have earned.
Learn from Wisdom
20: Undisciplined people find Wisdom's demands too hard and don't have enough determination to meet them.
Learn from Wisdom
21: Her requirements are a burden heavier than they are willing to bear, and they quickly lay them aside.
Learn from Wisdom
22:
“Discipline” means just that — discipline,
 and not many people are able to discipline themselves.
Learn from Wisdom
6.22 Hebrew “Discipline”… discipline; Greek “Wisdom”… wisdom.
23: My child, listen to me and take my advice.
Learn from Wisdom
24: Put Wisdom's chains round your feet and her yoke round your neck.
Learn from Wisdom
25: Carry her on your shoulder and don't be resentful of her bonds.
Learn from Wisdom
26: Follow Wisdom, and keep to her ways with all your heart.
Learn from Wisdom
27: Go looking for her, and she will reveal herself to you. Take hold of her and don't let go.
Learn from Wisdom
28: Then you will discover the peace of mind she offers, and she will become your joy.
Learn from Wisdom
29-30: The signs of slavery you wear will become signs of royal majesty. Her chains will be your protection, and you will wear her yoke like a magnificent robe.
31: You will wear Wisdom like a splendid crown.
Learn from Wisdom
32: My child, you can learn if you want to. You can be clever if you are determined to be.
33: If you are willing to listen, you will learn and become wise.
34: Go where the old people are; find someone who is wise, and stay with him.
35: Be ready to listen when religious people speak, and don't miss anything that shows insight.
36: If you find someone with understanding, get up early to call on him; wear out his doorstep with your visits.
37: Devote all your time to studying the Lord's commands and thinking about them. He will give you the insight and wisdom you are looking for.

1/1-2: If you do no wrong, no wrong will ever come to you.
1-2: If you do no wrong, no wrong will ever come to you.
3: Do not plough the ground to sow seeds of injustice; you may reap a bigger harvest than you expect.
Miscellaneous Advice
4: Don't ask the Lord for a place of honour or ask the king for an important position.
Miscellaneous Advice
5: Don't try to convince the Lord that you are righteous or make a show of your wisdom before the king.
Miscellaneous Advice
6: Don't set your heart on being a judge, unless you have the strength of character needed to put an end to injustice. If you let yourself be influenced by someone in a position of power, your integrity will be damaged.
Miscellaneous Advice
7: Don't commit any crime against the general public, and don't disgrace yourself among your fellow-citizens.
Miscellaneous Advice
8: Don't commit the same sin twice. The punishment you get the first time ought to be enough.
Miscellaneous Advice
9: Don't think that God Most High is going to take into account how very generous you are and so accept any offering you decide to make.
Miscellaneous Advice
10: Never get tired of praying, and never miss a chance to give to the poor.
Miscellaneous Advice
11: Don't laugh at someone who has been humiliated. It is the Lord who humbles a person, but the Lord also raises him up again.
Miscellaneous Advice
12: Don't think up lies to tell about your friends.
Miscellaneous Advice
13: Don't tell lies at all. It never does any good.
Miscellaneous Advice
14: In an official assembly, don't get up and talk a lot of nonsense. And don't repeat yourself when you pray.
Miscellaneous Advice
15: Don't try to avoid farm work or other hard labour; the Most High has given us these jobs to do.
Miscellaneous Advice
16: Don't join forces with a crowd of sinners; remember that the Lord's punishment is sure to come.
Miscellaneous Advice
17: Be very humble, because the decay of death awaits us all.
Relations with Others
7.17 Hebrew the decay… all; Greek the punishment of the ungodly is fire and worms.
18: Don't betray a friend for money. Don't betray a real friend for all the gold in the world.
Relations with Others
19: Don't miss your chance to marry a wise and good woman. A gracious wife is worth more than gold.
Relations with Others
20: Don't ill-treat servants who do their work well, or employees who do their best for you.
Relations with Others
21:
Show the same love to wise servants that you would show to yourself,
 and let them have their freedom.
Relations with Others
7.21 Hebrew Show the same love… yourself; Greek Show love to a wise servant.; Ex 21.2; Deut 15.12–15
22: Take good care of any animals you own. If they make money for you, keep them.
Relations with Others
23: If you have sons, educate them. Teach them self-discipline while they are young.
Relations with Others
24: If you have daughters, keep them virtuous, and don't be too indulgent with them.
Relations with Others
25: When you give your daughter in marriage, you have finished a great task, but give her to a sensible man.
Relations with Others
26: If you have a good wife, do not divorce her, but do not trust yourself to someone you don't love.
Relations with Others
27: Honour your father with all your heart, and never forget how your mother suffered when you were born.
Relations with Others
28: Remember that you owe your life to them. How can you ever repay them for all they have done for you?
Relations with Others
29: Fear the Lord with all your heart, and have respect for his priests.
Relations with Others
30: Love your Creator with all your strength, and give his ministers your support.
Relations with Others
31: Honour the Lord and respect the priests. Supply them with what you have been commanded to give: the first produce, the offering for sin, the shoulder of the sacrificial animal — all the required sacrifices and offerings.
Relations with Others
32: Give your help to the poor, and the Lord will give you his perfect blessing.
33: Be generous to every living soul, and be gracious to the memory of the dead.
34: Show sympathy to those who have lost a loved one, and mourn with them.
35: Do not hesitate to visit the sick. You will be loved for things like these.
36: Whatever you do, remember that some day you must die. As long as you keep this in mind, you will never sin.

1: Do not challenge a person who has influence; you may fall into his power.
Using Common Sense
2: Do not quarrel with someone rich; he may bribe people to turn against you. Gold has destroyed many people and corrupted kings.
Using Common Sense
3: Don't argue with someone who talks too much; you will just be adding fuel to his fire.
Using Common Sense
4: Don't make fun of someone who has bad manners; he may insult your ancestors.
Using Common Sense
5: Don't criticize someone if he is already turning away from sin. Remember that we are all guilty.
Using Common Sense
6: Never think less of someone because he is old; some of us are growing old, too.
Using Common Sense
7: Don't be happy over the death of anyone; remember that all of us must die.
Using Common Sense
8: Do not disregard what the wise have said. Study their proverbs; from them you can learn to be cultured and to serve great people.
Using Common Sense
9: Pay attention to what old people say, for they learnt from those who came before them. You can learn from them, and they can teach you how to have an answer ready when you need one.
Using Common Sense
10: You should not provide a sinner with more opportunity to sin. You might get hurt yourself; it's as easy as getting burnt while adding logs to a fire.
Using Common Sense
11: You should not get up and walk out on someone who is insolent. He might twist your words and use them against you.
Using Common Sense
12: You should not lend anything to someone more powerful than you. If you do, you might as well consider it lost.
Using Common Sense
13: You should not guarantee anyone else's loan to an extent that you cannot afford. You must be prepared to pay any such guarantee.
Using Common Sense
14: You should not bring a lawsuit against a judge. He will win because of his position.
Using Common Sense
15: You should not travel with anyone who is reckless. It will cause you nothing but trouble. He will do any foolish thing he pleases, and you will die with him.
Using Common Sense
16: You should not get into an argument with anyone who has a hot temper or go to a place where the two of you will be alone. Violence means nothing to such a person, and he will attack you when there is no one to help you.
Using Common Sense
17: You should not ask a stupid person for advice. He will not be able to keep secret what you tell him.
Using Common Sense
18: You should not do anything in front of a stranger that should be kept secret. There is no way of knowing what will happen if you do.
Using Common Sense
19: You should not reveal your most private thoughts to anyone. If you do, you may as well throw away any chance of happiness.
Using Common Sense
8.19 Hebrew If… happiness; Greek And don't let anyone do you a favour.

1: Don't be jealous of the wife you love. You will only be teaching her how to do you harm.
Women
2: Do not surrender your dignity to a woman.
Women
3:
Keep away from other men's wives
 or they will trap you.
Women
9.3 Hebrew other men's wives; Greek immoral women.
4: Don't keep company with female musicians; they will trick you.
Women
5: Don't look too intently at a virgin, or you may find yourself forced to pay a bride price.
Women
6: Don't give yourself to prostitutes, or you may lose everything you own.
Women
7: So don't go looking about in the streets or wandering round in the run-down parts of town.
Women
8: When you see a good-looking woman, look the other way; don't let your mind dwell on the beauty of any woman who is not your wife. Many men have been led astray by a woman's beauty. It kindles passion as if it were fire.
Women
9: Don't sit down to eat with another man's wife or join her for a drink. You may give in to the temptation of her charms and be destroyed by your passion.
Friendship
10: Never abandon an old friend; you will never find a new one who can take their place. Friendship is like wine; it gets better as it grows older.
Friendship
11: Don't be jealous of a sinner's success; you don't know what kind of disaster is in store for him.
Friendship
12: Don't take pleasure in the things that make ungodly people happy; remember that they will be held guilty as long as they live.
Friendship
13: If you keep away from someone who has the power to put you to death, you will not have to fear for your life; but if you must go near him, be very careful, or he may kill you. Be conscious that you are walking among hidden traps, that you are an easy target.
Friendship
14: Get to know the people round you as well as you can, and take advice only from those who are qualified to give it.
Friendship
15: Engage in conversation with intelligent people, and let the Law of the Most High be the topic of your discussions.
Friendship
16: Choose righteous people for your dinner companions. Your chief pride should be your fear of the Lord.
Rulers
17: Skilled workers are admired for the things they make, and leaders' wisdom is proved by their words.
Rulers
18: Someone who speaks rashly and recklessly is feared and hated by everyone in town.
Rulers

1: A wise ruler will educate his people, and his government will be orderly.
2: All the officials and all the citizens will be like their ruler.
3: An uneducated king will ruin his people, but a government will grow strong if its rulers are wise.
4: The Lord sees to the government of the world and brings the right person to power at the right time.
5: The success of that person is in the Lord's hands. The Lord is the source of the honour given to any official.
Pride
6: Don't be angry with someone for every little thing he does wrong. Don't do anything out of injured pride.
Pride
7: Arrogance and injustice are hated by both the Lord and human beings.
Pride
8: Injustice, arrogance, and wealth cause nations to fall from power, and others then rise to take their place.
Pride
9: We are only dust and ashes; what have we got to be proud of? Our body decays even while we are alive.
Pride
10.9 Our body… alive; this sentence in both Greek and Hebrew is unclear.
10:
A long illness puzzles the doctor.
 Even a king may be alive today and dead tomorrow.
Pride
10.10 A long… doctor; this sentence in both Greek and Hebrew is unclear.

26.18 strong ankles; some manuscripts have firm breasts or determined heart.; 26.18 Some manuscripts add verses 19–27: My child, stay healthy while you are young, and don't give your strength to strangers. 20 Search the whole land for a fertile field, and plant it with your own seed, trusting your own good stock. 21 Then your children will survive and grow up confident of their good family. 22 A prostitute is like spittle; a married woman who has affairs brings death to her lovers. 23 A lawless man will get a godless wife, as he deserves, but a man who honours the Lord will have a devout wife. 24 A shameless wife enjoys making a disgrace of herself, but a modest wife will act modestly even alone with her husband. 25 A self-willed woman is a bitch, but a woman with a sense of decency honours the Lord. 26 A wife who honours her husband will seem wise to everyone; but if she dishonours him by her overbearing attitude, everyone will know that she is ungodly. Betraying Secrets

1: The Lord is taking note of your sins, and if you take vengeance on someone, the Lord will take vengeance on you.

Arguments

Arguments

Vicious Talk

1: Be kind enough to lend to your neighbour when he needs help. You are keeping the Lord's commands if you help him.

1: Foolish people are deceived by vain hopes, and dreams get them all excited.

These are the Edomites,

I, Jesus son of Sirach Eleazar
 of Jerusalem, put all my wisdom into writing this book to provide instruction and knowledge for others.

The Wisdom of Jesus Son of Sirach

13: When I was still young, before I started out on my travels, I boldly prayed for Wisdom.
The Search for Wisdom
14: I went to the Temple and asked for her, and I will look for her as long as I live.
The Search for Wisdom
15: From my blossoming youth to my ripe old age she has been my delight. I have followed directly in her path ever since I was young.
The Search for Wisdom
16: I received Wisdom as soon as I began listening for her, and I have been rewarded with great knowledge.
The Search for Wisdom
17: I have always been a learner and am grateful to everyone who has been my teacher.
The Search for Wisdom
18: I was determined to live wisely and was devoted to the cause of goodness. I have no regrets.
The Search for Wisdom
19: I fought for Wisdom and was strict in my conduct. When I prayed, I sadly confessed how far short of Wisdom I fell.
The Search for Wisdom
20: But I was determined to have her, and I found her by keeping myself free from sin. I have grown in Wisdom since first I found her, and I will never be without her.
The Search for Wisdom
21: Because I was driven by the desire to find her, I have been richly rewarded.
The Search for Wisdom
22: The Lord gave me a gift for words, and I have used it in his praise.
The Search for Wisdom
23: Come to me, all you that need instruction, and learn in my school.
The Search for Wisdom
24: Why do you admit that you are ignorant and do nothing about it?
The Search for Wisdom
25: This is what I say: It costs nothing to be wise.
The Search for Wisdom
26: Put on the yoke, and be willing to learn. The opportunity is always near.
The Search for Wisdom
27: See for yourselves! I have really not studied very hard, but I have found great contentment.
The Search for Wisdom
28: No matter how much it costs you to get Wisdom, it will be well worth it.
The Search for Wisdom
29: Be joyfully grateful for the Lord's mercy, and never be ashamed to praise him.
The Search for Wisdom
30: Do your duty at the proper time, and the Lord, at the time he thinks proper, will give you your reward.
